Serplulimab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, serplulimab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2) expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Serplulimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

pegdinetanib is an adnectin which represents a new format, alternative to monoclonal antibody, by using the fibronectin domain F10 as a scaffold, developed by Adnexus. Pegylation site: Cys C93. Doubly methoxy-PEGylated maleimide derivative, with a molecular mass of 40 kDaI. Adnexus is a Bristol-Myers Squibb R&D Company, located at the BMS Waltham facility, Massachusetts U.S.A. It focused on the discovery and development of a new class of targeted biologics called Adnectins™. Adnexus was formed in 2002 and was acquired by Bristol-Myers Squibb Company (BMS) in 2007. Mode of action. Adnectins are small proteins (10 to 12 kDa) that are derived from the 10th type III domain of human fibronectin (10Fn3). Adnectins share with antibody variable domains a beta-sheet sandwich fold with diversified loops, but differ from antibodies in primary sequence and have a simpler, single-domain structure without disulfide bonds. As a consequence, Adnectins bind targets with affinity and specificity as high as those of antibodies, but are easier to manipulate genetically and compatible with bacterial expression systems. Garivulimab targets and binds to programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274,PD-L1), an immunosuppressive ligand expressed on tumor cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to CD274, garivulimab blocks its binding to its receptor programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1) expressed on activated T cells, leaving the PDCD1 pathway intact through its second ligand, PDCD1LG2 (PD-L2), which is less expressed in tumor cells, to maintain inhibitory signals mediated by PDCD1LG2/PDCD1. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Garivulimab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with one mutation in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v4 CH2 A114) to reduce FcγR binding and antibody-dependent cytoxicity (ADCC), preventing T cells depletion.

Tislelizumab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, tislelizumab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) expressed on tumor cells, and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2), primarily expressed on antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Tislelizumab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding. In addition, tislelizumab has five mutations in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G4v37 CH2 P1.4, V1.3, A1.2, A27, V92) to reduce FcγR binding and antibody-dependent cytoxicity (ADCC) or complement-dependent cytoxicity (CDC), preventing T cells depletion by any residual ADCC activity; and one mutation in the CH3 domain (IMGT variant G4v6CH3 K88) to confer more stability.
The binding of an anti-PD-1 antibody to FcγRΙ has a profound impact on its biological functions.

Ipilimumab targets and binds to cytotoxic T-lymphocyte-associated antigen 4 (CTLA4), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on T cells that downregulates both proliferation and activation of T cells. Once bound to CTLA4, ipilimumab blocks its binding to its ligand CD80/CD86 molecules and promotes CD80/CD86 binding to CD28 co-stimulatory receptor. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by CTLA4 signaling and promotes stimulatory CD28 pathway resulting in expansion and activation of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Ipilimumab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and induce antibody-dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity (ADCC) for Treg cells depletion.

Selicrelumab targets and binds to CD40, a stimulatory immunoreceptor expressed on antigen-presenting cells (APCs) and certain types of tumor cells. Once bound to CD40, selicrelumab promotes CD40 signaling. This activation results in activation and expansion of the target cells (i.e. B cells, dendritic cells (DCs), and macrophages) which activate IG class switching in B cells and enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Selicrelumab is an IgG2-Kappa antibody wich presents a strong agonist effect through CD40 aggregation, due to its lack of flexibility in the hinge region, and limited FcγR-binding to prevent APCs depletion.

The IDENTIFICATION axiom of the Formal IMGT-ONTOLOGY or IMGT-Kaleidoscope postulates that molecules, cells, tissues, organs, organisms or populations, their processes and relations, have to be identified. The IDENTIFICATION axiom has generated the concepts of identification which provide the terms and rules to identify an entity, its processes and its relations. In molecular biology, the concepts of identification allow to identify the molecular components, the molecules, their processes and their relations at the genome, transcriptome, proteome, structure and function levels.

Retifanlimab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, retifanlimab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2) expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Retifanlimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Zalifrelimab targets and binds to cytotoxic T-lymphocyte-associated antigen 4 (CTLA4), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on T cells that downregulates both proliferation and activation of T cells. Once bound to CTLA4, zalifrelimab blocks its binding to its ligand CD80/CD86 molecules and promotes CD80/CD86 binding to CD28 co-stimulatory receptor. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by CTLA4 signaling and promotes stimulatory CD28 pathway resulting in expansion and activation of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Zalifrelimab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and induce antibody-dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity (ADCC) for Treg cells depletion.

Tremelimumab targets and binds to cytotoxic T-lymphocyte-associated antigen 4 (CTLA4), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on T cells that downregulates both proliferation and activation of T cells. Once bound to CTLA4, tremelimumab blocks its binding to its ligand CD80/CD86 molecules and promotes CD80/CD86 binding to CD28 co-stimulatory receptor. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by CTLA4 signaling and promotes stimulatory CD28 pathway resulting in expansion and activation of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Tremelimumab is an IgG2-Kappa antibody with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Durvalumab targets and binds to programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274,PD-L1), an immunosuppressive ligand expressed on tumor cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to CD274, durvalumab blocks its binding to its receptor programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1) expressed on activated T cells, leaving the PDCD1 pathway intact through its second ligand, PDCD1LG2 (PD-L2), which is less expressed in tumor cells, to maintain inhibitory signals mediated by PDCD1LG2/PDCD1. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Durvalumab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with three mutations in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v39 CH2 F1.3, E1.2, S116) to reduce FcγR binding and antibody-dependent cytoxicity (ADCC) or complement-dependent cytoxicity (CDC), preventing T cells depletion.

Lodapolimab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, lodapolimab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2) expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Lodapolimab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with five mutations in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v43-60 CH2 A1.3, E1.2, A1, S115, S116) to reduce FcγR binding and antibody-dependent cytoxicity (ADCC) or complement-dependent cytoxicity (CDC), preventing T cells depletion.

Variable domains (V-BETA and V-ALPHA) are individualized (different for each patient) and are not provided in the sequence. Mode of action (MOA): tacatresgene autoleucel recognize and bind to tumor cells expressing the targeted neoantigen, resulting in a cytotoxic T-lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated immune response against the patient's tumor cells. Mutated cancer-specific proteins displayed on a patient cancer cell surface (or 'neoantigens') contain 'neoepitopes' (neoE), which are specifically recognized by T cells within the patient's own immune response. The imPACT Isolation Technology® allows to identify and capture these rare neoE-specific CD8+ T cells from peripheral blood. Polyclonal autologous neoE TR sequences of these cells are extracted and engineered, and by CRISPR/Cas9 technology, used to replace natural TR in autologous fresh CD8+ and CD4+ T cells for re-injection in the patient.

Mitazalimab targets and binds to CD40, a stimulatory immunoreceptor expressed on antigen-presenting cells (APCs) and certain types of tumor cells. Once bound to CD40, mitazalimab promotes CD40 signaling. This activation results in activation and expansion of the target cells (i.e. B cells, dendritic cells (DCs), and macrophages) which activate IG class switching in B cells and enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Mitazalimab is an IgG1-Kappa antibody with ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and may induce antibody-dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity (ADCC) against tumor cells.

The human anti-CD40 agonist antibody mitazalimab (ADC-1013; JNJ-64457107) activates antigen-presenting cells, improves expansion of antigen-specific T cells, and enhances anti-tumor efficacy of a model cancer vaccine in vivo.

Ieramilimab targets and binds to lymphocyte activation gene 3 (LAG3, CD223), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to LAG3, ieramilimab blocks its binding to its ligands expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APC). This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by the LAG3 signaling resulting in activation and proliferation of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response. Ieramilimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Envafolimab targets and binds to programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274,PD-L1), an immunosuppressive ligand expressed on tumor cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to CD274, envafolimab blocks its binding to its receptor programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1) expressed on activated T cells, leaving the PDCD1 pathway intact through its second ligand, PDCD1LG2 (PD-L2), which is less expressed in tumor cells, to maintain inhibitory signals mediated by PDCD1LG2/PDCD1. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Envafolimab is a single VH domain fused to an IgG1-Fc antibody with one mutation in the hinge region (IMGT variant G1v37 h S5) and two mutations in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v71 CH2 A27, G116) to reduce FcγR binding and antibody-dependent cytoxicity (ADCC) or complement-dependent cytoxicity (CDC), preventing T cells depletion.

Phase describes the level of a trial required of drugs before (and after) they are routinely used in clinical practice: - Phase I trials assess toxic effects on humans (not many people participate in them, and usually without controls); - Phase ll trials assess therapeutic benefit (usually involving a few hundred people, usually with controls, but not always); - Phase III trials compare the new treatment against standard (or placebo) treatment (usually a full randomised controlled trial). At this point, a drug can be approved for community use. - Phase IV monitors a new treatment in the community, often to evaluate longterm safety and effectiveness. [Glossary of Terms in The Cochrane Collaboration] A trial can be of a combination phase (e.g., I/II). The concept of phase is not applicable to trials studying certain interventions (e.g., device, procedure, behavioral)

Lucatumumab targets and binds to CD40, a stimulatory immunoreceptor expressed on antigen-presenting cells (APCs) and certain types of tumor cells. Once bound to CD40, lucatumumab blocks its binding to its ligand CD40LG expressed on activated T cells. This blockade results in inhibition of proliferation of the malignant B cells. Lucatumumab is an IgG1-Kappa antibody with ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and induce antibody-dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity (ADCC) against tumor cells.

Iparomlimab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, iparomlimab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2) expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Iparomlimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Ocrelizumab targets and binds to MS4A1 (membrane-spanning 4-domains subfamily A member 1, CD20), a B cell surface molecule with a role in the development and differentiation of B cells into plasma cells. Once bound to MS4A1, ocrelizumab depletes circulating CD20+ B cells by apoptosis. Ocrelizumab is an IgG1-kappa antibody able to induce complement-dependent cytotoxicity (CDC), antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC) and antibody-dependent cellular phagocytosis (ADCP) to indirectly deplete CD20+ expressing cells.

Tocilizumab targets and binds to interleukin 6 receptor (IL6R), a protein complex consisting of this protein and interleukin 6 signal transducer (IL6GT/gp130) a cytokine responsible for participating in the processes of inflammation and the maturation of B cells. Once bound to IL6R, tocilizumab effectively obstructs IL6-IL6R signaling, encompassing both the classic and trans IL6 signaling pathways through the Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitors. This interference leads to the prevention of both anti-inflammatory (phosphoinositide-3-kinase (PI3K) and protein kinase B / PKB pathway) and pro-inflammatory responses (signal transducer and activator of transcription 3-STAT3 and MAPK (mitogen-activated protein kinase) pathway). Tocilizumab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and induce antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC).

Toripalimab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, toripalimab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2) expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Toripalimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Crovalimab targets and binds to the C5 molecule, a component of the innate immune system that plays an important role in inflammation, host homeostasis, and host defense against pathogens. Once bound to C5, crovalimab blocks the advancement of the complement cascade irrespective of the triggers. This action inhibits the splitting of C5, consequently preventing the generation of C5a and membrane attack complex (MAC) molecules. As a result, this intervention obstructs and/or restrains inflammation and cellular damage caused by the complement system. Crovalimab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with six mutations in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v94 CH2 R1.2, R1.1, K3, G110, S115, S116) that reduce antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC) or complement-dependent cytotoxicity (CDC), two mutations in the CH3 domain (G1v85 CH3 L107, A114) introduced to increase FcRn and antibody half-life and two other mutations in the CH3 domain (G1v100 CH3 R118, E120) that decrease the Rheumatoid factor (RF) binding Fc variants with enhanced FcRn binding.

Blocks cell entry of  R5 strains of HIV-1 by binding to CCR5. Macrophage (M-tropic) strains of HIV-1, or non-syncitia-inducing strains (NSI), use the CCR5 for cell entry and are able to replicate in macrophages and CD4+ T cells. These strains are called R5. CCR5 is used by almost all primary HIV-1 isolates regardless of viral genetic subtype. CCR5 is a coreceptor as it contributes with CD4 to HIV entry. Its normal ligands are CCL3 (MIP-1-alpha), CCL4 (MIP-1-beta), CCL5 (RANTES) and CCL8 (MCP-2) which are able to suppress HIV-1 infection in vitro.

Olendalizumab targets and binds to the C5 molecule, a component of the innate immune system that plays an important role in inflammation, host homeostasis, and host defense against pathogens and particularly on the alpha chain protein, C5a anaphylatoxin, which possesses potent spasmogenic and chemotactic activity. Once bound to C5, olendalizumab blocks the advancement of the complement cascade irrespective of the triggers. This action inhibits the splitting of C5, consequently preventing the generation of C5a and membrane attack complex (MAC) molecules. As a result, this intervention obstructs and/or restrains inflammation and cellular damage caused by the complement system. Olendalizumab is an IgG2-IgG4-kappa hybrid antibody that reduces complement-dependent cytotoxicity (CDC) and FcγR effector properties.

Levilimab targets and binds to interleukin 6 receptor (IL6R), a protein complex consisting of this protein and interleukin 6 signal transducer (IL6GT/gp130) a cytokine responsible for participating in the processes of inflammation and the maturation of B cells. This interference leads to the prevention of both anti-inflammatory (phosphoinositide-3-kinase (PI3K) and protein kinase B / PKB pathway) and pro-inflammatory responses (signal transducer and activator of transcription 3-STAT3 and MAPK (mitogen-activated protein kinase) pathway). Levilimab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with three mutations in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v76 CH2 P1.4, V1.3, A1.2) that reduce FcyR binding and antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC), and three other mutations in the CH2 domain (G1v21 CH2 Y15.1, T16, E18) to increase the FcRn binding and its half-life. Zeluvalimab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, zeluvalimab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2) expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Zeluvalimab is an IgG1-kappa aglycosylated antibody with one mutation in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v29 CH2 A84.4) with an additional disulfide bridge to stabilize the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v54 CH2 C83, C85), resulting in loss of FcyR binding ability, to prevent T cells depletion.

On November 9, 2020,  Food and Drug Administration (FDA) issued an Emergency Use Authorization (EUA) for emergency use of bamlanivimab for the treatment of mild to moderate coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) in adult and pediatric patients. Press release . Bamlanivimab is the first neutralising monoclonal antibody to receive emergency use authorisation from the FDA as a treatment for mild to moderate Covid-19, 94 days after the monoclonal antibody discovery workflow began for the molecule. This represents the shortest timeline from discovery to public usage for a monoclonal antibody to date.
On February 9, 2021, Food and Drug Administration (FDA) issued an Emergency Use Authorization (EUA) for emergency use of bamlanivimab and etesevimab to be administered together for the treatment of mild to moderate coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) in adults and pediatric patients. Press release . Bamlanivimab and etesevimab are neutralizing IgG1 monoclonal antibodies that bind to distinct but overlapping epitopes within the receptor binding domain of the spike protein of SARS-CoV-2. They are both investigational drugs and are not currently approved for any indication. On February 25, 2021, FDA reissued the February 9, 2021 letter.
On April 16, 2021, Food and Drug Administration (FDA) revoked Emergency Use Authorization (EUA) for bamlanivimab, when administered alone. Revocation letter .

Betifisolimab targets and binds to programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274,PD-L1), an immunosuppressive ligand expressed on tumor cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to CD274, betifisolimab blocks its binding to its receptor programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1) expressed on activated T cells, leaving the PDCD1 pathway intact through its second ligand, PDCD1LG2 (PD-L2), which is less expressed in tumor cells, to maintain inhibitory signals mediated by PDCD1LG2/PDCD1. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Satralizumab targets and binds to interleukin 6 receptor (IL6R), a protein complex consisting of this protein and interleukin 6 signal transducer (IL6GT/gp130) ta cytokine responsible for participating in the processes of inflammation and the maturation of B cells. Once bound to IL6R, satralizumab effectively obstructs IL6-IL6R signaling, encompassing both the classic and trans IL6 signaling pathways through the Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitors. This interference leads to the prevention of both anti-inflammatory (phosphoinositide-3-kinase (PI3K) and protein kinase B/PKB pathway) and pro-inflammatory responses (signal transducer and activator of transcription 3-STAT3 and MAPK (mitogen-activated protein kinase) pathway). Satralizumab is an IgG2-kappa antibody with limited FcγR-binding.

Favezelimab targets and binds to lymphocyte activation gene 3 (LAG3, CD223), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to LAG3, favezelimab blocks its binding to its ligands expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APC). This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by the LAG3 signaling resulting in activation and proliferation of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response. Favezelimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Sotigalimab targets and binds to CD40, a stimulatory immunoreceptor expressed on antigen-presenting cells (APCs) and certain types of tumor cells. Once bound to CD40, sotigalimab promotes CD40 signaling. This activation results in activation and expansion of the target cells (i.e. B cells, dendritic cells (DCs), and macrophages) which activate IG class switching in B cells and enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Sotigalimab is an IgG1-Kappa antibody with one mutation in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v35 CH2 E29) to enhance FcγRIIb binding promoting antibody crosslinking and strong CD40 signaling while reducing the binding to FcγRIIIa and inhibiting ADCC activity on APCs.

Pacmilimab targets and binds to programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274,PD-L1), an immunosuppressive ligand expressed on tumor cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to CD274, pacmilimab blocks its binding to its receptor programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1) expressed on activated T cells, leaving the PDCD1 pathway intact through its second ligand, PDCD1LG2 (PD-L2), which is less expressed in tumor cells, to maintain inhibitory signals mediated by PDCD1LG2/PDCD1. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Pacmilimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion. In addition, pacmilimab is designed to be preferentially activated by proteases in the tumor microenvironment (TME).

Ravulizumab targets and binds to the C5 molecule, a component of the innate immune system that plays an important role in inflammation, host homeostasis, and host defense against pathogens. Once bound to C5, ravulizumab blocks the advancement of the complement cascade irrespective of the triggers. This action inhibits the splitting of C5, consequently preventing the generation of C5a and membrane attack complex (MAC) molecules. As a result, this intervention obstructs and/or restrains inflammation and cellular damage caused by the complement system. Ravulizumab is an IgG2-IgG4-kappa hybrid antibody that reduces complement-dependent cytotoxicity (CDC) and FcγR effector properties. In addition, ravulizumab has three mutations in the CH3 domain (IMGT variant G4v24 CH3 L107, S114) to increase FcRn binding and antibody half-life.

Once bound to PDCD1, zimberelimab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2) expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Zimberelimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

The "Subgroup" concept allows to classify a subset of genes which belong to the same group, and which, in a given species, share at least 75% of identity at the nucleotide sequence level (and in the germline configuration for the V, D, and J genes).

The CLASSIFICATION axiom of the Formal IMGT-ONTOLOGY or IMGT-Kaleidoscope postulates that, for molecular components, any molecule and its relations have to be classified. IMGT-ONTOLOGY concepts of classification generated from the CLASSIFICATION axiom led to the IMGT® standardized IG and TR gene and allele nomenclature.

The "Clan" concept allows to classify genes which belong to a set of subgroups, eventually from different species, and which appear related on phylogenetic trees. IMGT clans correspond to the categorization of variable gene subgroups based on nucleotide sequences similarity.

The "Gene" concept allows to classify a unit of DNA sequence which can be potentially transcribed and/or translated (this definition includes the regulating elements in 5' and 3', and introns, if present). The Gene leafconcepts are named by the gene names. In IMGT-ONTOLOGY, a gene name is composed of the name of the species (or the short name encoded by 6 or 9 characters) and of the international HGNC/IMGT gene symbol. By extension, orphons and pseudogenes are also Gene concepts.

The "Group" concept allows to classify a set of genes which belong to the same multigene family, within the same species or between different species. For the IG and TR, the set of genes is identified by a GeneType (variable, diversity, joining or constant).

MOA: effect analog to THPO (thrombopoeitin)
romiplostim, a Fc-peptide fusion protein, is a thrombopoietin receptor agonist. It belongs to the thrombopoietin (TPO) mimetic class, although the peptides have no homology with TPO. By binding to the thrombopoietin receptor (MPL, CD110), it activates the differentiation of the megacaryocytes and therefore increases the platelet production.
Romiplostim is a thrombopoietin mimetic Fc-fusion peptide. The sequences of the peptides are different from the natural ligand THPO (thrombopoietin, megakaryocyte growth and differentiation factor, MPLLG, MPL ligand, c-mpl ligand, megakaryocyte growth and development factor, megakaryocyte colony-stimulating factor).  
Romiplostim binds and activates MPL (thrombopoietin receptor, myeloproliferative leukemia virus oncogene, MPL proto-oncogene, TPOR, CD110) and stimulates JAK2 (Janus kinase 2) and STAT5A (signal transducer and activator of transcription 5A) pathways. This leads to megakaryocyte proliferation and differentiation. Megakaryocytes then extend protrusions (proplatelets) into the bone marrow blood vessels. Sheer stress from blood flow fragments the protrusions, creating platelets.

Adnectins are small proteins (10 to 12 kDa) that are derived from the 10th type III domain of human fibronectin (10Fn3). Adnectins share with antibody variable domains a beta-sheet sandwich fold with diversified loops, but differ from antibodies in primary sequence and have a simpler, single-domain structure without disulfide bonds. As a consequence, Adnectins bind targets with affinity and specificity as high as those of antibodies, but are easier to manipulate genetically and compatible with bacterial expression systems. The "GeneType" concept allows to identify the type of gene.

It comprises six leafconcepts. 'conventional_with_leader' and 'conventional_without_leader' identify any (coding or not coding) gene, with or without leader L region (or signal peptide), respectively, other than the immunoglobulin (IG) or T cell receptor (TR) genes of the vertebrate adaptive immune response. The other four leafconcepts refer to the IG and TR genes and are specific to immunogenetics: three of these leafconcepts, 'variable' (V), 'diversity' (D) and 'joining' (J) identify the IG and TR genes that rearrange at the DNA level in the B and T cells and code the V, D and J regions, respectively, of the IG and TR variable domains; the fourth leafconcept, 'constant' (C), identifies the IG  and TR genes that code the C region of the IG and TR chains.

Budigalimab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Avelumab targets and binds to programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274,PD-L1), an immunosuppressive ligand expressed on tumor cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to CD274, avelumab blocks its binding to its receptor programmed cell death 1  (PDCD1, PD-1) expressed on activated T cells, leaving the PDCD1 pathway intact through its second ligand, PDCD1LG2 (PD-L2), which is less expressed in tumor cells, to maintain inhibitory signals mediated by PDCD1LG2/PDCD1. Sarilumab targets and binds to interleukin 6 receptor (IL6R), a protein complex consisting of this protein and interleukin 6 signal transducer (IL6GT/gp130) a cytokine responsible for participating in the processes of inflammation and the maturation of B cells. Once bound to IL6R, sarilumab effectively obstructs IL6-IL6R signaling, encompassing both the classic and trans IL6 signaling pathways through the Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitors. This interference leads to the prevention of both anti-inflammatory (phosphoinositide-3-kinase (PI3K) and protein kinase B / PKB pathway) and pro-inflammatory responses (signal transducer and activator of transcription 3-STAT3 and MAPK (mitogen-activated protein kinase) pathway). Sarilumab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and induce antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC).

Orilanolimab targets and binds to FCGRT, the neonatal Fc receptor (FcRn) to recycle IgG in the serum. Once bound to FCGRT, orilanolimab outcompetes endogenous IgG binding leading to increased IgG catabolism, resulting in a reduction in the available IgGs. Orilanolimab is a hinge stabilized IgG4-kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcyR binding ability, to prevent antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC).
Blocking FcRn in humans reduces circulating IgG levels and inhibits IgG immune complex-mediated immune responses.

Cemiplimab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, cemiplimab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) expressed on tumor cells, and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2), primarily expressed on antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Cemiplimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Tesidolumab targets and binds to the C5 molecule, a component of the innate immune system that plays an important role in inflammation, host homeostasis, and host defense against pathogens. Once bound to C5, tesidolumab blocks the advancement of the complement cascade irrespective of the triggers. This action inhibits the splitting of C5, consequently preventing the generation of C5a and membrane attack complex (MAC) molecules. As a result, this intervention obstructs and/or restrains inflammation and cellular damage caused by the complement system. Tesidolumab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with two mutations in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v14 CH2 A1.3, A1.2) that reduce FcγR binding and antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC) or complement-dependent cytotoxicity (CDC).

Socazolimab targets and binds to programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274,PD-L1), an immunosuppressive ligand expressed on tumor cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to CD274, socazolimab blocks its binding to its receptor programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1) expressed on activated T cells, leaving the PDCD1 pathway intact through its second ligand, PDCD1LG2 (PD-L2), which is less expressed in tumor cells, to maintain inhibitory signals mediated by PDCD1LG2/PDCD1. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Socazolimab is an IgG1-lambda antibody with ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and induce antibody-dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity (ADCC) for potential enhanced efficacy in certain tumor types.

Fianlimab targets and binds to lymphocyte activation gene 3 (LAG3, CD223), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Blocks cell entry of  R5 strains of HIV-1 by binding to CCR5. Macrophage (M-tropic) strains of HIV-1, or non-syncitia-inducing strains (NSI), use the CCR5 for cell entry and are able to replicate in macrophages and CD4+ T cells. These strains are called R5. CCR5 is used by almost all primary HIV-1 isolates regardless of viral genetic subtype. CCR5 is a coreceptor as it contributes with CD4 to HIV entry. Its normal ligands are CCL3 (MIP-1-alpha), CCL4 (MIP-1-beta), CCL5 (RANTES) and CCL8 (MCP-2) which are able to suppress HIV-1 infection in vitro.

Vobarilizumab is a bispecific antibody fragment (VH-VH’) that simultaneously binds to interleukin 6 receptor (IL6R) and albumin. IL6R is a protein complex consisting of this protein and interleukin 6 signal transducer (IL6GT/gp130) a cytokine responsible for participating in the processes of inflammation and the maturation of B cells. Albumin is a protein that regulates the colloid osmotic pressure of blood plasma and acts as a carrier protein for a wide range of endogenous molecules. Once bound to IL6R, vobarilizumab effectively obstructs IL6-IL6R signaling, encompassing both the classic and trans IL6 signaling pathways through the Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitors. This interference leads to the prevention of both anti-inflammatory (phosphoinositide-3-kinase (PI3K) and protein kinase B/PKB pathway) and pro-inflammatory responses (signal transducer and activator of transcription 3-STAT3 and MAPK (mitogen-activated protein kinase) pathway). The binding to albumin increases the half-life of the antibody fragment.

Once bound to CD40, bleselumab blocks CD40-CD40LG co-stimulatory pathway signaling. This blockage results in hindering humoral and cellular immune responses. Bleselumab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding. Dalnicastobart targets and binds to CD40, a stimulatory immunoreceptor expressed on antigen-presenting cells (APCs) and certain types of tumor cells. Once bound to CD40, dalnicastobart promotes CD40 signaling. This activation results in activation and expansion of the target cells (i.e. B cells, dendritic cells (DCs), and macrophages) which activate IG class switching in B cells and enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Dalnicastobart is an IgG1-Kappa antibody with one mutation in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v47 CH2 delG1.1) to enhance FcyRIIb binding promoting antibody crosslinking and strong CD40 signaling while reducing the binding to FcγRIIIa and inhibiting ADCC activity on APCs.

ibalizumab is currently being developed by TaiMed Biologics but was originally developed by Tanox Inc. (Houston, TX, USA), now part of Genentech. As part of Genentech's takeover of Tanox, the patent for ibalizumab was sold to TaiMed Biologics, a biotech company formed in 2007 in Taiwan. Mode of action. ibalizumab binds CD4, the primary receptor for HIV, and inhibits HIV from entering cells. It is a post-attachment inhibitor, blocking HIV from binding to the CCR5 and CXCR4 co-receptors after HIV binds to CD4 on the surface of a CD4+ T cell. By targeting HIV-1 entry before coreceptor binding and fusion have taken place, ibalizumab acts broadly on CCR5 and CXCR4 tropic strains.
ibalizumab and MH2 proteins bind CD4 on different epitopes localized on [D2] and [D1], respectively, and Ibalizumab does not appear to interfere with CD4-dependent immunity. (Emu B. et al .Phase 3 Study of ibalizumab for Multidrug-Resistant HIV-1.

Eculizumab targets and binds to the C5 molecule, a component of the innate immune system that plays an important role in inflammation, host homeostasis, and host defense against pathogens. Once bound to C5, eculizumab blocks the advancement of the complement cascade irrespective of the triggers. This action inhibits the splitting of C5, consequently preventing the generation of C5a and membrane attack complex (MAC) molecules. As a result, this intervention obstructs and/or restrains inflammation and cellular damage caused by the complement system. Eculizumab is an IgG2-IgG4-kappa hybrid antibody that reduces complement-dependent cytotoxicity (CDC) and FcγR effector properties.

Obexelimab targets and binds to CD19 antigen, a member of the immunoglobulin superfamily (IgSF). CD19 is continuously and stably expressed on all stages of B lineage differentiation on the surface of B cell lymphocytes. Once bound to CD19, obexelimab depletes circulating CD19+ B cells, thus suppressing inflammatory responses and impairing B-cell-dependent T-cell activation. Obexelimab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and induce antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC) and complement-dependent cytotoxicity (CDC). for enhanced efficacy against plasma cells and auto-antibodies production. In addition, obexelimab has two mutations in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v25 CH2 E29, F113) to increase FcγRIIb binding resulting in inhibition by downstream ITIM signaling in B cells.

Dacetuzumab targets and binds to CD40, a stimulatory immunoreceptor expressed on antigen-presenting cells (APCs) and certain types of tumor cells. Once bound to CD40, dacetuzumab promotes CD40 signaling. This activation results in activation and expansion of the target cells (i.e. B cells, dendritic cells (DCs), and macrophages) which activate IG class switching in B cells and enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Dacetuzumab is an IgG1-Kappa antibody with ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and may induce antibody-dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity (ADCC) against tumor cells.

ARGX-113 is a recombinant Fc portion of a human IgG1 monoclonal antibody, mutated at 5 amino acid residues to increase affinity for human FcRn receptor (IGHG1v22 CH2 Y15.1, T16, E18, CH3 K113, F114, H116). ARGX-113 encompasses residues D220-K447 and has been modified with ABDEG™ technology, a technology that uses an antibody to enhance IgG degradation. efgartigimod (ARGX-113) is a Fc engineered to bind with higher affinity and reduced pH dependence to FcRn, blocking IgG recycling and increasing IgG clearance. ARGX-113 is an antibody Fc-fragment based on argenx' ABDEG™ technology. ABDEG™ mutations dramatically increase the Fc/FcRn binding both at neutral and acidic pH. This results in constitutive blockage of FcRn function by ARGX-113, and increases IgG clearance.

Ziltivekimab targets and binds to interleukin 6 (IL6), a cytokine  responsible for participating in the processes of inflammation and the maturation of B cells.  Once bound to IL6, ziltivekimab effectively obstructs IL6-IL6R signaling, encompassing both the classic and trans IL6 signaling pathways through the Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitors. This interference leads to the prevention of both anti-inflammatory (phosphoinositide-3-kinase (PI3K) and protein kinase B / PKB pathway) and pro-inflammatory responses (signal transducer and activator of transcription 3-STAT3 and MAPK (mitogen-activated protein kinase) pathway).  Ziltivekimab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with three mutations in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v21 CH2 Y15.1, T16, E18) to increase the FcRn binding and its half-life. IMGT proposed MOA on 23/08/2023.

Sugemalimab targets and binds to programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274,PD-L1), an immunosuppressive ligand expressed on tumor cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to CD274, sugemalimab blocks its binding to its receptor programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1) expressed on activated T cells, leaving the PDCD1 pathway intact through its second ligand, PDCD1LG2 (PD-L2), which is less expressed in tumor cells, to maintain inhibitory signals mediated by PDCD1LG2/PDCD1. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Sugemalimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Immunoglobulins (or antibodies) are proteins capable of specific recognition and binding with an antigen. Antibodies carries antigen-binding sites that bind non-covalently with the corresponding antigen epitope. Antibodies are produced in the body by the B lymphocytes at the cell surface and are secreted by plasma cells, in response to stimulation by antigen.

An antibody or immunoglobulin monomer is formed by two identical light chains and two identical heavy chains. There are five classes of immunoglobulins in human, IgM, IgD, IgG, IgA and IgE, each with distinct heavy chains (mu, delta, gamma, alpha and epsilon, respectively).
When expressed at the surface of the B cells, immunoglobulins are anchored by their heavy chains. These membrane immunoglobulins (mIgM, mIgD, mIgG, mIgA and mIgE) are associated with CD79A (Ig-alpha, mb-1) and CD79B (Igbeta, B29). One CD79A-CD79B heterodimer and one immunoglobulin monomer constitute the B cell receptor (BcR).
When secreted by the plasmocytes, immunoglobulins are monomeric (IgG, IgD, IgE, and IgA in serum), dimeric (IgA in seromucous secretions), or pentameric (IgM).

Adebrelimab targets and binds to programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274,PD-L1), an immunosuppressive ligand expressed on tumor cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to CD274, adebrelimab blocks its binding to its receptor programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1) expressed on activated T cells, leaving the PDCD1 pathway intact through its second ligand, PDCD1LG2 (PD-L2), which is less expressed in tumor cells, to maintain inhibitory signals mediated by PDCD1LG2/PDCD1. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Adebrelimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding. In addition, adebrelimab has two mutations in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G4v4 CH2 CH2 A1.3, A1.2) to reduce any residual FcγR binding and antibody-dependent cytoxicity (ADCC) or complement-dependent cytoxicity (CDC), preventing T cells depletion.
IMGT proposed MOA on 29/11/2022.

Siltuximab targets and binds to interleukin 6 (IL6), a cytokine responsible for participating in the processes of inflammation and the maturation of B cells. Once bound to IL6, siltuximab effectively obstructs IL6-IL6R signaling, encompassing both the classic and trans IL6 signaling pathways through the Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitors. This interference leads to the prevention of both anti-inflammatory (phosphoinositide-3-kinase (PI3K) and protein kinase B / PKB pathway) and pro-inflammatory responses (signal transducer and activator of transcription 3-STAT3 and MAPK (mitogen-activated protein kinase) pathway). Siltuximab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and induce antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC).

Inebilizumab targets and binds to CD19 antigen, a member of the immunoglobulin superfamily (IgSF). CD19 is continuously and stably expressed on all stages of B lineage differentiation on the surface of B cell lymphocytes. Once bound to CD19, inebilizumab depletes circulating CD19+ B cells, thus suppressing inflammatory responses and impairing B-cell-dependent T-cell activation. Inebilizumab is an IgG1-kappa afucosylated antibody with increased ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and induce antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC) and complement-dependent cytotoxicity (CDC) for enhanced efficacy against plasma cells and auto-antibodies production. Pembrolizumab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, pembrolizumab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) expressed on tumor cells, and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2), primarily expressed on antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Pembrolizumab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Mezagitamab targets and binds to CD38 molecule, a type II transmembrane glycoprotein expressed in immune cells, including natural killer cells, monocytes, and activated T cells and plasma cells. Once bound to CD38, mezagitamab dysregulates the CD38 enzymatic function and contributes directly to cell kill. Mezagitamab is a human IgG1-lambda antibody able to induce complement-dependent cytotoxicity (CDC), antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC) and antibody-dependent cellular phagocytosis (ADCP) to indirectly deplete CD38+ expressing cells.

Giloralimab targets and binds to CD40, a stimulatory immunoreceptor expressed on antigen-presenting cells (APCs) and certain types of tumor cells. Once bound to CD40, giloralimab promotes CD40 signaling. This activation results in activation and expansion of the target cells (i.e. B cells, dendritic cells (DCs), and macrophages) which activate IG class switching in B cells and enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Giloralimab is an IgG1-Kappa antibody with one mutation in the CH2 domain (IMGT variant G1v72 CH2 E37) to enhance FcγRIIb binding promoting antibody crosslinking and strong CD40 signaling while reducing the binding to FcγRIIIa and inhibiting ADCC activity on APCs.

Pidilizumab in the treatment of diffuse large B-cell lymphoma.

Pidilizumab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, pidilizumab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2) expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Pidilizumab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and may induce antibody-dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity (ADCC).

Miptenalimab targets and binds to lymphocyte activation gene 3 (LAG3, CD223), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to LAG3, miptenalimab blocks its binding to its ligands expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APC). This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by the LAG3 signaling in resulting activation and proliferation of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response. Miptenalimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Efgartigimod alfa targets and binds to FCGRT, the neonatal Fc receptor (FcRn) to recycle IgG in the serum. Once bound to FCGRT, efgartigimod alfa outcompetes endogenous IgG binding leading to increased IgG catabolism, resulting in a reduction in the available IgGs. Efgartigimod alfa is an IgG1 Fc fragment (Fc-gamma1) with three mutations in the CH2 domain and two mutations in the CH3 domain (IMGT variant G1v96 CH2 Y15.1, T16, E18; CH3 K113, F114) to increase the FcRn binding (without pH dependency) and its half-life.

Vilobelimab targets and binds to the C5 molecule, a component of the innate immune system that plays an important role in inflammation, host homeostasis, and host defense against pathogens. Once bound to C5, vilobelimab blocks the advancement of the complement cascade irrespective of the triggers. This action inhibits the splitting of C5, consequently preventing the generation of C5a and membrane attack complex (MAC) molecules. As a result, this intervention obstructs and/or restrains inflammation and cellular damage caused by the complement system. Vilobelimab is an IgG4-kappa antibody with limited FcγR-binding. Sintilimab: A Promising Anti-Tumor PD-1 Antibody.

Sintilimab targets and binds to programmed cell death 1 (PDCD1, PD-1), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. Once bound to PDCD1, sintilimab blocks its binding to its ligand programmed cell death ligand 1 (CD274, PD-L1) and programmed cell death ligand 2 (PDCD1LG2, PD-L2) expressed on tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells (APCs). PDCD1LG2 is less expressed than CD274 in tumor cells and APCs. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by PDCD1 signaling resulting in activation and expansion of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response against tumor cells. Sintilimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Encelimab targets and binds to lymphocyte activation gene 3 (LAG3, CD223), an inhibitory immunoreceptor expressed on activated T cells that downregulates both activation and proliferation of T cells. This blockade reverses the inactivation of T cells caused by the LAG3 signaling resulting in activation and proliferation of T cells as well as enhancement of cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL)-mediated anti-tumor immune response. Encelimab is a hinge-stabilized IgG4-Kappa antibody (IMGT variant G4v5 h P10) with limited FcγR-binding to prevent T cells depletion.

Gefurulimab is a bispecific antibody fragment (VH-VH’) that simultaneously binds to a C5 molecule and an albumin. The C5 molecule is a component of the innate immune system that plays an important role in inflammation, host homeostasis, and host defense against pathogens. Albumin is a protein that regulates the colloid osmotic pressure of blood plasma and acts as a carrier protein for a wide range of endogenous molecules. Once bound to C5, gefurulimab blocks the advancement of the complement cascade irrespective of the triggers. This action inhibits the splitting of C5, consequently preventing the generation of C5a and membrane attack complex (MAC) molecules. As a result, this intervention obstructs and/or restrains inflammation and cellular damage caused by the complement system. The binding to albumin increases the half-life of the antibody fragment. IMGT proposed MOA on 21/08/2023.

Sirukumab targets and binds to interleukin 6 (IL6), a cytokine  responsible for participating in the processes of inflammation and the maturation of B cells. Once bound to IL6, sirukumab effectively obstructs IL6-IL6R signaling, encompassing both the classic and trans IL6 signaling pathways through the Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitors. This interference leads to the prevention of both anti-inflammatory (phosphoinositide-3-kinase (PI3K) and protein kinase B/PKB pathway) and pro-inflammatory responses (signal transducer and activator of transcription 3-STAT3 and MAPK (mitogen-activated protein kinase) pathway). Sirukumab is an IgG1-kappa antibody with ability to bind to FcyRIIIa and induce antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC). IMGT proposed MOA on 23/08/2023.

IMGT-ONTOLOGY is the first ontology for immunogenetics and immunoinformatics and the global reference in the domain. It was created as early as in 1989 to manage the complexity of immunogenetics data in IMGT®, the international ImMunoGeneTics information system® (https://www.imgt.org). IMGT-ONTOLOGY provides a semantic specification of the terms to be used in immunogenetics and immunoinformatics and manages the related knowledge, thus allowing the standardization for immunogenetics data from genome, proteome, genetics, two-dimensional (2D) and three-dimensional (3D) structures.

IMGT-ONTOLOGY manages the immunogenetics knowledge through diverse facets that rely on the seven axioms of the Formal IMGT-ONTOLOGY, or IMGT-Kaleidoscope: "IDENTIFICATION", "CLASSIFICATION", "DESCRIPTION", "NUMEROTATION", "LOCALIZATION", "ORIENTATION" and "OBTENTION". These axioms postulate that any object, any process and any relation have to be identified, classified, described, numbered, localized and orientated, and that the way it is obtained can be characterized. As the same axioms can be used to generate concepts for multi-scale level approaches, the Formal IMGT-ONTOLOGY represents a paradigm for system biology ontologies, which need to identify, to classify, to describe, to number, to localize and to orientate objects, processes and relations at the molecule, cell, tissue, organ, organism or population levels.
